fields.description:
## What
DEPRECATE list-epics in favor of generic list/query, by first EXPANDING the generic list filters to cover what list-epics filters by, then making list-epics a deprecating alias.

list-epics' filters/criteria all derive from the same reducer+graph the generic reads use; only the FILTER EXPOSURE is missing from generic list. So:

1. Expand generic 'list' filters (reads.py list_states + ticket_reads CLI + library list_tickets + MCP list tool):
   - --min-children=N : tickets with >= N non-deleted direct children (child count via parent_id over reduced state).
   - --unblocked / --blocked : filter by blocking state (reuse rebar.graph find_ready_tickets / all_blockers_closed).
   These are universal filters, useful beyond epics.
2. Deprecate list-epics: emit a deprecation warning at every surface (CLI stderr, library DeprecationWarning, MCP tool description) steering to 'rebar list --type=epic --status=open,in_progress [--unblocked] [--min-children=N]'.
3. FOR NOW keep list-epics' current behavior + output ({p0_bugs,epics} envelope, child counts) intact so the MCP outputSchema + consumers don't break; the bespoke ticket-list-epics.sh (453 LOC) is removed in a later step once consumers migrate.

p0_bugs is a separable bug metric ('list --type=bug --priority=0'); not part of the generic list expansion.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] generic 'list' gains --min-children + --unblocked/--blocked at CLI + library + MCP (parity with list-epics' filter criteria)
- [ ] new filters covered by tests (dual-impl where relevant)
- [ ] list-epics emits a deprecation warning at CLI (stderr) + library + MCP, steering to generic list; behavior/output otherwise unchanged
- [ ] existing list-epics suites still green (behavior preserved); deprecation warning does not pollute stdout/JSON
- [ ] docs note the deprecation + the new generic filters
